title: TC-ACCT-001: Verify Log Out button transitions to Log In immediately after logout on Account tab
steps: Ensure user is logged in (Account tab shows user profile info and 'Log Out' button).
Tap the Account tab at the bottom navigation bar.
Wait for the logout API call to complete (observe loading indicator if present).
Observe the button text and UI state on the Account tab immediately after logout completes.
Do NOT navigate away from the Account tab.
expected: The 'Log Out' button should immediately change to 'Log In' (or 'Sign In') button on the Account tab without requiring the user to leave and re-enter the screen. The Account tab should reflect the logged-out state (e.g., no user profile info displayed, generic login prompt visible).

title: TC-ACCT-002: Verify Log Out → Log In button state persists after app backgrounding and foregrounding
steps: Ensure user is logged in (Account tab shows 'Log Out' button).
Verify the button has changed to 'Log In' on the Account tab.
Press the Home button to background the app.
Re-open the app from the recent apps list (foreground the app).
expected: The Account tab should still display the 'Log In' button after the app is backgrounded and foregrounded. The logged-out state should persist. The button should NOT revert to 'Log Out'.

title: TC-ACCT-003: Verify Log Out → Log In button state persists after app force-kill and relaunch
steps: Ensure user is logged in (Account tab shows 'Log Out' button).
Verify the button has changed to 'Log In' on the Account tab.
Force-kill the app (swipe away from recent apps).
expected: The Account tab should display the 'Log In' button. The logged-out state should persist across app restart. The user should not appear logged in.

title: TC-ACCT-004: Verify Log Out button state does NOT change until API callback completes (negative timing test)
steps: Ensure user is logged in (Account tab shows 'Log Out' button).
Immediately (before the logout API call completes) observe the button text.
Wait for the logout API call to complete.
expected: The button text should remain 'Log Out' until the logout API call completes. After the API callback succeeds, the button should change to 'Log In'. The button should NOT change to 'Log In' prematurely before the API confirms logout.

title: TC-ACCT-005: Verify Log In button transitions back to Log Out after logging in again on Account tab
steps: Ensure user is logged out (Account tab shows 'Log In' button).
Complete the login flow (enter credentials and authenticate).
Wait for the login API call to complete.
expected: After successful login, the 'Log In' button should change to 'Log Out' button on the Account tab. User profile information should be displayed. No navigation away and back should be required to see the updated state.

title: TC-ACCT-006: Verify Log Out → Log In button state persists after navigating to another tab and back
steps: Ensure user is logged in (Account tab shows 'Log Out' button).
Verify the button has changed to 'Log In' on the Account tab.
Tap the Devices tab (or another bottom navigation tab).
Tap the Account tab again to re-enter.
expected: The Account tab should still display the 'Log In' button upon re-entry. The button should not flicker or temporarily show 'Log Out' before updating. The logged-out state should be consistent before and after tab navigation.

title: TC-ACCT-007: Verify Log Out button state after Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect cycle following logout
steps: Ensure user is logged in and a Poly device is connected via Bluetooth.
Verify the button has changed to 'Log In'.
Disconnect the Bluetooth device (turn off Bluetooth or device).
expected: The Account tab should still display the 'Log In' button after Bluetooth disconnect and reconnect. The Bluetooth state change should not cause the Account tab to revert to the logged-in state.

title: TC-ACCT-008: Verify Log Out button state during device rotation on Account tab
steps: Ensure user is logged in (Account tab shows 'Log Out' button).
Verify the button has changed to 'Log In'.
Rotate the phone to landscape orientation.
Rotate the phone back to portrait orientation.
expected: The Account tab should display the 'Log In' button in both landscape and portrait orientations after rotation. The button state should survive configuration changes (activity recreation).
